description: Add a directional time filter: block LONG entries during hours 3-9 UTC (hour_sin>=0.75 zone). The deadliest regime in the data is longs during non-Asian hours â€” hour_cos<0.63 AND direction>=0.50 yields only 15.4% WR (0.52x), and hour_sin>=0.75 AND direction>=0.50 yields 18.5% (0.63x). Gold tends to sell off during late-Asian/early-London as Asian physical demand fades and London dealers mark down â€” SSS short setups capture this flow, but longs during this window fight the dominant order flow. This requires a new config parameter (sss.block_long_hours_utc) and a small code addition to the entry filter.
expected_improvement: Eliminates the ~54 long trades in the 3-9 UTC window that had 18.5% WR, redirecting capital to the 40% WR regime (hour_sin<0.75 + low risk). Expected portfolio WR lift from 29.5% to ~33-35%. Combined with Hypothesis 1, the surviving trades should average 38-40% WR â€” enough to shift the strategy from marginally profitable to clearly positive expectancy at 2R reward.
